GPT-OSS-120B (low-effort mode) also generates the shorter reasoning traces forming 2% of the SFT data for the new Low-effort reasoning mode.
GPT-OSS-120B paraphrased SecQue-derived templates and produced GenSelect candidate answers for the 366k Financial Reasoning SFT pairs.
GPT-OSS-120B served as a generator in the CUDA SFT data pipeline alongside DeepSeek-R1.
gpt-oss-120b was used in the Conversational Tool Use 6-stage SFT pipeline.
GPT-OSS-120B operating in low-effort mode generated the low-effort reasoning samples (math, STEM QA, instruction following) that constitute 2% of Nemotron 3 Super's SFT data.
GPT-OSS-120B used as a CUDA SFT teacher (alongside DeepSeek-R1) and as the source of low-effort reasoning traces (~2% of SFT data) that train the low-effort reasoning mode.

GPT-OSS-120B is co-teacher (with DeepSeek-R1) for the CUDA SFT corpus and additionally generates the low-effort reasoning traces (~2% of SFT data, in low-effort mode) that train Super3's low-effort reasoning mode.
GPT-OSS-120B contributed CUDA kernel SFT samples and (via low-effort mode) shorter reasoning traces used in Nemotron 3 Super SFT.

GPT-OSS-120B paraphrased SecQue template instantiations into up to three reformulations per combination and produced five candidate answers per question with distinct seeds for the GenSelect best-of-N step.
GPT-OSS-120B generated CUDA C++ kernels, repair traces, and natural-language specifications for the 100K-sample CUDA SFT dataset.
GPT-OSS-120B was used in stages of the six-stage Conversational Tool Use SDG pipeline.
GPT-OSS-120B (in low-effort mode) generated training samples covering math reasoning, STEM QA, and instruction-following that introduce a low-effort reasoning mode into Nemotron 3 Super during SFT.
GPT-OSS-120B served as a teacher for two SFT subsets: CUDA kernel data (jointly with DeepSeek-R1) and the new low-effort reasoning traces (~2% of SFT data) collected from its low-effort mode.

edges.subject: nvidia/NVIDIA-Nemotron-3-Super-120B-A12B-BF16
edges.description: Nemotron-Math-Proofs-v1 (~580k NL proof problems, ~550k Lean 4 formalizations, ~900k verified proof trajectories) is SFT training data for the Nemotron-3-Super-120B BF16 master chat.
description_variants: Nemotron-Math-Proofs-v1 (~580k NL proof problems, ~550k Lean 4 formalizations, ~900k verified proof trajectories) is SFT training data for the Nemotron-3-Super-120B BF16 master chat.
SFT includes Nemotron-Math-Proofs-v1 'lean' subset (weight 2.0) for the Formal Proofs domain (mathematical proof generation). Reused from Nano3.
Formal-proofs SFT subset 'lean' (weight 2.0) — Lean4 mathematical proof generation traces.

NVIDIA-Nemotron-3-Super-120B-A12B-BF16 was post-trained on nvidia/Nemotron-Math-Proofs-v1 as a 0.68% mixing-ratio component of the RLVR 1 stage blend (split `rlvr1`, 138,712 samples) within Nemotron-3-Super-RL-Training-Blends.

NVIDIA-Nemotron-3-Super-120B-A12B-BF16 was post-trained on nvidia/Nemotron-Math-Proofs-v1 as a 0.75% mixing-ratio component of the RLVR 2 stage blend (split `rlvr2`, 156,278 samples) within Nemotron-3-Super-RL-Training-Blends.

NVIDIA-Nemotron-3-Super-120B-A12B-BF16 was post-trained on nvidia/Nemotron-Math-Proofs-v1 as a 0.88% mixing-ratio component of the RLVR 3 stage blend (split `rlvr3`, 107,037 samples) within Nemotron-3-Super-RL-Training-Blends.

Nemotron-3-Super-120B-A12B is RL-trained at the RLVR 1 stage on a blend (rlvr1.jsonl split of nvidia/Nemotron-3-Super-RL-Training-Blends) in which nvidia/Nemotron-Math-Proofs-v1 contributes 0.68% of the mixture.

Nemotron-3-Super-120B-A12B is RL-trained at the RLVR 2 stage on a blend (rlvr2.jsonl split of nvidia/Nemotron-3-Super-RL-Training-Blends) in which nvidia/Nemotron-Math-Proofs-v1 contributes 0.75% of the mixture.

Nemotron-3-Super-120B-A12B is RL-trained at the RLVR 3 stage on a blend (rlvr3.jsonl split of nvidia/Nemotron-3-Super-RL-Training-Blends) in which nvidia/Nemotron-Math-Proofs-v1 contributes 0.88% of the mixture.
Super3 SFT trains on the lean subset of Nemotron-Math-Proofs-v1 (w=2.0) for Lean theorem-proving and formal mathematical proof generation.

Nemotron-3-Super-120B-A12B was trained on nvidia/Nemotron-Math-Proofs-v1 as part of the RLVR 1 RL training stage blend, with mixing ratio 0.68%. The blend (split 'rlvr1') contains 138,712 samples (6.7G).

Nemotron-3-Super-120B-A12B was trained on nvidia/Nemotron-Math-Proofs-v1 as part of the RLVR 2 RL training stage blend, with mixing ratio 0.75%. The blend (split 'rlvr2') contains 156,278 samples (8.1G).

Nemotron-3-Super-120B-A12B was trained on nvidia/Nemotron-Math-Proofs-v1 as part of the RLVR 3 RL training stage blend, with mixing ratio 0.88%. The blend (split 'rlvr3') contains 107,037 samples (4.5G).
SFT included the lean subset of nvidia/Nemotron-Math-Proofs-v1 (weight 2.0) for formal-proof generation, reused from Nano3.
Nemotron-Math-Proofs-v1 component (0.68%) of the rlvr1 split for Super-120B-A12B RL training.
Nemotron-Math-Proofs-v1 component (0.75%) of the rlvr2 split for Super-120B-A12B RL training.
Nemotron-Math-Proofs-v1 component (0.88%) of the rlvr3 split for Super-120B-A12B RL training.
Nemotron-Math-Proofs-v1 (large Lean-4 formal proof dataset) underpins the new formal-proof verification environment introduced in Super 120B-A12B Instruct's RLVR.

edges.subject: nvidia/NVIDIA-Nemotron-3-Super-120B-A12B-BF16
edges.description: Post-training pipeline (chat template, two-stage SFT, multi-environment RLVR + SWE-RL + RLHF + MTP healing, reasoning-budget control) follows the Nemotron 3 Nano recipe with stronger emphasis on agentic tasks and scaled-up environments.
description_variants: Post-training pipeline (chat template, two-stage SFT, multi-environment RLVR + SWE-RL + RLHF + MTP healing, reasoning-budget control) follows the Nemotron 3 Nano recipe with stronger emphasis on agentic tasks and scaled-up environments.

Multi-environment RLVR strategy follows Nemotron 3 Nano: simultaneous training over all environments rather than staged by capability, found more stable and less prone to reward hacking and forgetting than staged approaches.

RLHF approach mirrors Nemotron 3 Nano's: training a principle-following GenRM (Wang et al. 2025b) rather than a vanilla GenRM, and using human preference data from Helpsteer3 + lmarena-140k. Difference: Super uses GenRM throughout RLVR stage too.

PivotRL methodology (Yi et al. 2026) and the four agentic domains (Agentic Programming, Search, Terminal Use, Conversational Tool Use) that use it derive from the Nemotron family's pivot-based agentic RL line of work.

GenSelect best-of-N answer-selection methodology (Toshniwal et al. 2025) is the same approach used family-wide; SecQue seed (BenYoash et al. 2025) provides the financial-reasoning template seeds. Inherited methodology, no weight or data inheritance from a single artifact.

Deliberative-alignment safety methodology (Guan et al. 2025) used family-wide; Super inherits the response-policy + content-moderation classifier safety pipeline from Nemotron 3 Nano with expanded synthetic prompts.
Nemotron 3 Super follows the same general post-training recipe as Nemotron 3 Nano (SFT then multi-env RLVR then SWE-RL then RLHF), with stronger agentic emphasis and scaled RL infrastructure.
Chat, Infinibyte, and Formal Proofs SFT datasets were reused unchanged from Nemotron 3 Nano post-training.
The line-by-line translation pipeline used to construct multilingual SFT data was reused directly from the Nemotron 3 Nano post-training recipe.
RLVR data mixture, curriculum (filter SFT-correct prompts, then difficulty-sort), and base recipe were adopted from Nemotron 3 Nano and scaled up.
RLHF stage approach (use GenRM throughout multi-env RL and run a separate RLHF-only stage at end) follows Nemotron 3 Nano's recipe with the addition of training the GenRM throughout multi-env RL.

anchor_list.excerpt: We follow the same general recipe as Nemotron 3 Nano, with a stronger emphasis on agentic tasks.
anchor_list.explanation: States Super post-training inherits Nemotron 3 Nano's recipe.

anchor_list.excerpt: We employ a unified RLVR strategy similar to Nemotron 3 Nano, but significantly scale the number of environments.
anchor_list.explanation: Inherits unified-RLVR methodology from Nemotron 3 Nano.

anchor_list.excerpt: We follow a similar approach to Nemotron 3 Nano for RLHF, training a large GenRM model to provide supervision during RL.
anchor_list.explanation: States RLHF approach inherits from Nemotron 3 Nano.

anchor_list.excerpt: We apply PivotRL for all agentic domains: including for Agentic Programming, Search, Terminal Use, and Conversational Tool Use.
anchor_list.explanation: Pivot RL is methodology adopted family-wide; Super inherits it for Nano and the broader Nemotron 3 line.

anchor_list.excerpt: We adopt the GenSelect strategy (Toshniwal et al., 2025)
anchor_list.explanation: Cites the GenSelect strategy methodology.

anchor_list.excerpt: We have significantly enhanced our safety framework compared to Nemotron 3 Nano
anchor_list.explanation: States Super's safety framework extends Nemotron 3 Nano's.

anchor_list.excerpt: We follow the same general recipe as Nemotron 3 Nano, with a stronger emphasis on agentic tasks.
anchor_list.explanation: Explicit recipe-inheritance statement from Nemotron 3 Nano.

anchor_list.excerpt: We reuse the following datasets from the Nemotron 3 Nano SFT datasets: Chat, Infinibyte, and Formal Proofs.

edges.subject: rl-research/DR-Tulu-SFT-8B
edges.description: rl-research/DR-Tulu-SFT-8B is the SFT checkpoint trained on the rl-research/dr-tulu-sft-data corpus, which packages curated prompts with full trajectories (reasoning traces, tool calls, and cited answers).
description_variants: rl-research/DR-Tulu-SFT-8B is the SFT checkpoint trained on the rl-research/dr-tulu-sft-data corpus, which packages curated prompts with full trajectories (reasoning traces, tool calls, and cited answers).

DR-Tulu-SFT-8B was supervised-fine-tuned on the publicly released rl-research/dr-tulu-sft-data mixture (~16K trajectories of long-form and short-form information-seeking tasks with GPT-5-generated tool-use traces).

DR-Tulu-SFT-8B is fine-tuned on the rl-research/dr-tulu-sft-data dataset of long-form information-seeking queries with deep-research trajectories; tool_output spans are masked from the loss (use_span_masking: true, mask_span_types: tool_output) so the model learns to emit tool calls but not memorize tool outputs.
DR-Tulu-SFT-8B was supervised fine-tuned on rl-research/dr-tulu-sft-data per the model card datasets field and body link to the HF dataset; trained for tool-use under the dr-agent-lib framework.

anchor_list.excerpt: This dataset contains the SFT training data for DR Tulu, containing prompts and full trajectories including reasoning traces, tool calls, and answers with citations.
anchor_list.explanation: States directly that this HF dataset is the SFT training data used for DR Tulu, making it the trained_on aggregator for the SFT-stage model rl-research/DR-Tulu-SFT-8B.

anchor_list.excerpt: we curated 16K SFT data points, including both short-form and long-form tasks. Appendix Table 8 shows statistics of our SFT data.
anchor_list.explanation: Paper reports the SFT data size and DR Tulu's HF release page hosts the dataset under rl-research/dr-tulu-sft-data.
Confirms the released SFT mixture used to train DR-Tulu-SFT-8B.

anchor_list.excerpt:
dataset: rl-research/dr-tulu-sft-data
template: qwen3
cutoff_len: 16384
use_span_masking: true 
mask_span_types: "tool_output"
anchor_list.explanation: The dataset field specifies the HF dataset id used for SFT, and the span-masking flags document how tool outputs are excluded from the loss.

anchor_list.excerpt: datasets: - rl-research/dr-tulu-sft-data ... This model has undergone SFT training on [this dataset](https://huggingface.co/datasets/rl-research/dr-tulu-sft-data).
anchor_list.explanation: Frontmatter datasets field and body sentence both name dr-tulu-sft-data as the SFT training corpus.

edges.subject: rl-research/DR-Tulu-SFT-8B
edges.description: DR-Tulu-SFT-8B is initialized from Qwen3-8B and continues training via supervised fine-tuning on 16K deep-research trajectories.
description_variants: DR-Tulu-SFT-8B is initialized from Qwen3-8B and continues training via supervised fine-tuning on 16K deep-research trajectories.

DR-Tulu-SFT-8B continues from the Qwen/Qwen3-8B base checkpoint via full fine-tuning (finetuning_type: full) with the qwen3 chat template; this is the warm start for the SFT stage that precedes the RL stage.
DR-Tulu-SFT-8B is initialized from Qwen3-8B as declared in the HF model card base_model frontmatter; SFT is then run on top of these weights for 5 epochs.

anchor_list.excerpt: We initialize our checkpoint with Qwen3-8B (Yang et al., 2025). For SFT, we run training on one H100 node (8 GPUs) and trained for 5 epochs
anchor_list.explanation: States explicitly that the SFT checkpoint is initialized from Qwen3-8B.
model_name_or_path identifies the base checkpoint that DR-Tulu-SFT-8B initializes from.

anchor_list.excerpt: base_model: Qwen/Qwen3-8B ... This is the SFT checkpoint of DR Tulu, an open deep research agent trained on top of [Qwen3-8B](https://huggingface.co/Qwen/Qwen3-8B).
anchor_list.explanation: Frontmatter base_model field and body text both pin Qwen3-8B as the SFT warm-start checkpoint.

edges.subject: rl-research/DR-Tulu-SFT-8B
edges.description: GPT-5 was the teacher model that produced the full deep-research trajectories (think tokens, tool calls, tool outputs, final answers) used as SFT supervision; GPT-5 was given a deep-research system prompt and the same google_search/paper_search/web_browse tool stack and emitted explicit mock thinking tokens before each tool call.

anchor_list.excerpt: We provide GPT-5 with a detailed system prompt that defines the deep research workflow and exposes a general web search tool, a paper search tool, and a web browsing tool, and ask it to produce the entire trajectory.
anchor_list.explanation: Paper names GPT-5 as the teacher generating full trajectories used for SFT.

edges.subject: rl-research/DR-Tulu-SFT-8B
edges.description: HotpotQA was sampled as a short-form, verifiable QA source mixed into the SFT mixture so DR Tulu retains concise-answer skills alongside long-form deep research.

anchor_list.excerpt: we sample from HotpotQA (Yang et al., 2018), TaskCraft (Shi et al., 2025), WebWalker-Silver (Wu et al., 2025a), and MegaScience (Fan et al., 2025)
anchor_list.explanation: Paper enumerates HotpotQA among the short-form, verifiable QA sources mixed into SFT.

edges.subject: rl-research/DR-Tulu-SFT-8B
edges.description: Additional challenging synthetic prompts for the SFT mixture were generated in the style of PopQA; the prompts were synthesized rather than copied, so PopQA contributed methodology rather than direct training rows.

anchor_list.excerpt: we generate additional challenging synthetic prompts inspired by PopQA (Mallen et al., 2023).
anchor_list.explanation: Paper attributes the synthetic prompt design to PopQA inspiration rather than direct inclusion.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: DR-Tulu-8B (RL) is initialized from the DR-Tulu-SFT-8B checkpoint and continues training with online RL via RLER (GRPO variant with token-level loss aggregation, sample packing, 1-step async tool calls, and KL=0.001).
description_variants: DR-Tulu-8B (RL) is initialized from the DR-Tulu-SFT-8B checkpoint and continues training with online RL via RLER (GRPO variant with token-level loss aggregation, sample packing, 1-step async tool calls, and KL=0.001).

DR-Tulu-8B is initialized from rl-research/DR-Tulu-SFT-8B (the prior SFT release) and continues training via GRPO + RLER preference/rubric optimization; the SFT checkpoint is the warm start for the RL stage.
DR-Tulu-8B is the RL checkpoint warm-started from rl-research/DR-Tulu-SFT-8B; both the frontmatter base_model field and body text declare DR-Tulu-SFT-8B as the initialization.

anchor_list.excerpt: We then further train DR Tulu (SFT) using RLER to allow it to explore and improve both its tool-use and answer capabilities, aided by the evolving rubrics described above.
anchor_list.explanation: Paper states the RL stage is initialized from the SFT checkpoint.
The model_name variable is the SFT checkpoint and is passed as --model_name_or_path to grpo_fast.py, identifying the warm-start of the RL run.

anchor_list.excerpt: base_model: - rl-research/DR-Tulu-SFT-8B ... This is the RL checkpoint of DR Tulu, an open deep research agent trained on top of [rl-research/DR-Tulu-SFT-8B].
anchor_list.explanation: Frontmatter base_model and body text both pin DR-Tulu-SFT-8B as the RL warm-start checkpoint.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: DR-Tulu-8B (RL) was RL-trained on the publicly released rl-research/dr-tulu-rl-data mixture comprising the long-form prompts and the persistent + evolving search-based rubrics generated during RLER training.
description_variants: DR-Tulu-8B (RL) was RL-trained on the publicly released rl-research/dr-tulu-rl-data mixture comprising the long-form prompts and the persistent + evolving search-based rubrics generated during RLER training.

DR-Tulu-8B's RLER stage trains on rl-research/dr-tulu-rl-data, a dataset of prompts paired with search-grounded rubrics (rubrics generated with GPT-4.1) sourced from OpenScholar and SearchArena prompts; the dataset_mixer_list_splits=train field selects the train split.

DR-Tulu-8B was RL-trained on rl-research/dr-tulu-rl-data per the model card datasets field and body link; training script published at github.com/rlresearch/dr-tulu/blob/rl/rl/open-instruct/train_dr_tulu.sh.

rl-research/dr-tulu-rl-data is the released RL training data (4,881 examples per the YAML front matter) used to train DR-Tulu-8B with RL, containing the OpenScholar and SearchArena prompt subsets paired with GPT-4.1-generated search-based rubrics.
DR-Tulu RL data with evolving rubric reward.

anchor_list.explanation: Confirms the released RL training data corresponds to DR Tulu's RLER prompt+rubric pool.

anchor_list.excerpt: We focus exclusively on long-form questions for RL training. Following the same LM-based filtering procedure ... we collect approximately 5K new prompts from SearchArena and OpenScholar.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: Approximately 5K filtered long-form prompts for RL training were drawn from SearchArena (in addition to OpenScholar), forming part of the RL prompt pool.
description_variants: Approximately 5K filtered long-form prompts for RL training were drawn from SearchArena (in addition to OpenScholar), forming part of the RL prompt pool.

Prompts in rl-research/dr-tulu-rl-data are sampled from SearchArena (lmarena-ai/search-arena-24k); via the rl-data mixture these prompts enter DR-Tulu-8B's RL training under the SearchArena-rubric subset.

The SearchArena prompts in DR-Tulu-8B's RL data were drawn from lmarena-ai/search-arena-24k (the LMArena Search Arena 24k crowdsourced search-augmented chat dataset); these prompts were paired with GPT-4.1-generated rubrics inside rl-research/dr-tulu-rl-data.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: GPT-4.1-mini (gpt-4.1-mini-2025-04-14) was the LM judge used to score every rollout against each persistent and evolving rubric (returning 0/0.5/1) and to compute the citation reward; its judgement governed inclusion of each rubric's signal in the GRPO reward, hence filtered_by.
description_variants: GPT-4.1-mini (gpt-4.1-mini-2025-04-14) was the LM judge used to score every rollout against each persistent and evolving rubric (returning 0/0.5/1) and to compute the citation reward; its judgement governed inclusion of each rubric's signal in the GRPO reward, hence filtered_by.

During RLER training the rubric reward signal that decides which on-policy rollouts are reinforced is computed by gpt-4.1-mini acting as the per-rubric LM judge (env var RUBRIC_JUDGE_MODEL=gpt-4.1-mini); the judge ranks/scores rollouts against the active rubrics rather than rewriting them.

anchor_list.excerpt: For rubric and citation scoring, we use GPT-4.1-mini (gpt-4.1-mini-2025-04-14) as an LM judge
anchor_list.explanation: Paper names GPT-4.1-mini as the LM judge that scores rollouts against rubrics for the RL reward.
RUBRIC_JUDGE_MODEL is the env var read by the RLER reward to invoke the LM rubric judge; gpt-4.1-mini is the global default judge for DR-Tulu-8B's RL run.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: GPT-4.1 served as the rubric generator G_rubric: it produced both the persistent initial search-based rubrics (after retrieving web/paper evidence per prompt) and the evolving positive/negative rubrics that compare current-policy rollouts during training; these LM-generated rubrics constitute the reward signal.
description_variants: GPT-4.1 served as the rubric generator G_rubric: it produced both the persistent initial search-based rubrics (after retrieving web/paper evidence per prompt) and the evolving positive/negative rubrics that compare current-policy rollouts during training; these LM-generated rubrics constitute the reward signal.

GPT-4.1 generated the per-prompt search-grounded rubrics that constitute the supervision signal in rl-research/dr-tulu-rl-data; DR-Tulu-8B's RL stage consumes this GPT-4.1-generated rubric content as training data, so the relation is generated_by (the rubrics themselves are content produced by GPT-4.1).

GPT-4.1 generated the search-based rubrics packaged in rl-research/dr-tulu-rl-data and consumed during DR-Tulu-8B's RL training (rubric content originates from GPT-4.1, paired with OpenScholar/SearchArena prompts).
GPT-4.1 also generates the rubric items used to grade rollouts; thus it generates training-time signal content.

anchor_list.excerpt: we use GPT-4.1 as an rubric generator for both initial and evolving rubrics.
anchor_list.explanation: Paper names GPT-4.1 as the rubric generator that produces the persistent and evolving rubrics defining the RL reward.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: DR-Tulu-SFT-8B is the team's own SFT-only intermediate checkpoint used as the RLER ablation comparison: holding tool stack and base data fixed, the RL run gains rubric coverage +8.2, answer precision +4.4, comprehensiveness +5.4, depth +6.5, citation precision +23.3 and citation recall +22.1 over the SFT checkpoint.

anchor_list.excerpt: Comparing DR Tulu (SFT) and DR Tulu (RL), we observe consistent gains from RLER across multiple aspects, including rubric coverage (+8.2 points), answer precision (+4.4), comprehensiveness (+5.4), and depth of response (+6.5).

anchor_list.position: Section 5.2 paragraph on RLER improves deep research quality

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: Qwen3-8B with the dr-agent-lib search stack ('Qwen3-8B + Our Search') is used as the no-DR-training ablation baseline: holding the inference pipeline fixed at the DR Tulu agent stack but skipping both SFT and RLER, isolating the contribution of the DR Tulu training recipe. DR Tulu-8B (RL) outperforms Qwen3-8B + Our Search by 31.8 average points on the long-form benchmark suite.

anchor_list.explanation: Tables 2 and 3 report the Qwen3-8B + Our Search ablation row alongside the DR Tulu-8B (RL) result.

edges.subject: rl-research/DR-Tulu-SFT-8B

anchor_list.explanation: Table 2 reports both rows enabling the SFT-vs-no-training ablation.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: The RLER reward design (per-question rubrics graded by an LM judge) was inspired by the ScholarQA-CS line of rubric-based long-form benchmarks; RLER extends this rubric-grading paradigm with search-grounded persistent rubrics and policy-co-evolving rubrics during online RL.

anchor_list.excerpt: Inspired by the success of evaluating long-form responses with detailed rubrics, including our ScholarQA-CS line of benchmarks, we address this with RLER.
anchor_list.explanation: Blog directly credits the ScholarQA-CS rubric-evaluation paradigm as RLER's inspiration.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b

edges.subject: allenai/Olmo-Hybrid-7B
edges.description: Hybrid-7B architecture is based on OLMo3 7B (TransformerConfig.olmo3_7B) with reduced attention heads and 3 GDN layers per 1 attention layer to match params/TPS for fair comparison.
description_variants: Hybrid-7B architecture is based on OLMo3 7B (TransformerConfig.olmo3_7B) with reduced attention heads and 3 GDN layers per 1 attention layer to match params/TPS for fair comparison.

Olmo-Hybrid-7B's architecture and training recipe are derived from Olmo-3-1025-7B (same TransformerConfig.olmo3_7B base, same Dolma 3 pretraining mix, same midtraining yaml as 32B sibling). The hybrid replaces 3-of-4 attention layers with GDN to study GDN+attention scaling vs the pure transformer baseline.

Architecture is built off `TransformerConfig.olmo3_7B(...)` and explicitly described as based on OLMo3 7B but with 2 fewer attention heads (30 vs 32) and reduced d_model so params/TPS match the pure-transformer baseline; trained from scratch (not weight-initialized from OLMo3 7B).
Hybrid base architecture is anchored to OLMo-3 7B with reduced attention heads to match params/TPS for fair comparison; not trained_from but used as architectural scaffold/comparison anchor.

Architecture is built off the olmo3_7B config (32 layers, 4096 d_model) with REMOVE_HEADS=2 (30 heads, 3840 d_model) and a 3:1 Gated-Delta-Net + attention block pattern. No weight inheritance — Hybrid is trained from scratch on the same Dolma 3 0925 mix as OLMo-3 32B.

OLMo-Hybrid-7B is architecturally derived from OLMo3 7B (config olmo3_7B with attention heads reduced from 32 to 30 and d_model adjusted to keep params/TPS comparable for fair-comparison ablation against the pure-transformer OLMo 3 7B).

anchor_list.explanation: README documents Hybrid-7B as an OLMo-3-7B-derived architecture with Gated Delta Net layers replacing 3 of every 4 attention layers.

edges.subject: allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-8B-DPO
edges.description: GPT-4o (gpt-4o-2024-0806) was the LLM-as-a-judge that rated each candidate response 1-5 across helpfulness, instruction-following, honesty, and truthfulness; ratings drove the chosen-vs-rejected pairing for the 8B preference mixture.
description_variants: GPT-4o (gpt-4o-2024-0806) was the LLM-as-a-judge that rated each candidate response 1-5 across helpfulness, instruction-following, honesty, and truthfulness; ratings drove the chosen-vs-rejected pairing for the 8B preference mixture.

Tulu 3 8B DPO's preference labels were obtained by GPT-4o-2024-08-06 acting as LLM-as-a-judge over four sampled responses per prompt, rating helpfulness/IF/honesty/truthfulness on a 1-5 scale; mean ratings binarized into chosen/rejected pairs.

Preference labels for Tülu 3 8B DPO are produced by GPT-4o (gpt-4o-2024-08-06) acting as the LLM-as-a-judge over four candidate responses per prompt; mean-rating binarization yields chosen/rejected pairs (Section 5.2.1 'Stage 3').

GPT-4o-2024-08-06 was the LLM-as-a-judge that rated 4 candidate completions per prompt across helpfulness/instruction-following/honesty/truthfulness; the highest-rated response became the chosen completion and a lower-rated one was sampled as rejected, producing the preference labels for this model's DPO mix.

GPT-4o-2024-08-06 was the LLM-as-a-judge that rated each candidate response (1–5 across helpfulness, instruction-following, honesty, truthfulness) and produced the binarized preference labels for the 8B DPO training pairs.

GPT-4o (gpt-4o-2024-08-06) acted as LLM-as-a-judge: rating each of the four sampled completions per prompt 1–5 along helpfulness/instruction-following/honesty/truthfulness, then binarizing into chosen/rejected pairs that became the 8B preference mixture used to DPO-tune allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-8B-DPO.

GPT-4o (gpt-4o-2024-08-06) is the LLM-as-a-judge that rated each of the four candidate responses 1-5 on helpfulness, instruction-following, honesty, and truthfulness; the highest-mean response was the chosen and a lower-mean was the rejected. This applies to every preference pair across the entire DPO mixture.
GPT-4o-2024-08-06 acts as the LLM-as-a-judge for Tulu 3 8B DPO preference-pair construction across the four UltraFeedback rating aspects; chosen vs. rejected derived from the mean rating.

edges.description: On-policy preference completions for the 8B mixture were generated by sampling from the 8B Tülu 3 SFT model itself (one of 22 models in the response-generation pool).
description_variants: On-policy preference completions for the 8B mixture were generated by sampling from the 8B Tülu 3 SFT model itself (one of 22 models in the response-generation pool).
On-policy preference responses (one of two responses per prompt) were generated by the Tulu 3 8B SFT model itself, then judged against off-policy generations.

allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-8B-SFT produced on-policy completions used in the chosen/rejected pairs of the 8B preference mixture for allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-8B-DPO (Stage 2 of the synthetic preference pipeline).
On-policy completions in the Tülu 3 8B preference mix are sampled from allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-8B-SFT per the synthetic preference pipeline in Section 5.2.1 'Response Generation'.

On-policy completions used in this preference mix (the SFT-Reused-On-Policy and WildChat-Reused-On-Policy splits) were sampled from allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-8B-SFT itself, then paired against off-policy completions from the model pool.
On-policy preference completions: one of the four candidates per prompt was sampled from Tülu 3 8B SFT (the SFT model that initializes this DPO run).
On-policy preference completions were generated by the Tulu 3 8B SFT model — paired against off-policy completions from external instruct models in the synthetic preference pipeline.

On-policy completions for allenai/llama-3.1-tulu-3-8b-preference-mixture were sampled from allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-8B-SFT (the Tülu 3 8B SFT model) and paired against off-policy outputs to produce the chosen/rejected pairs for DPO.

Tülu 3 8B SFT generates the on-policy half of preference pairs (Stage 2 of §5.2.1): for the on-policy subset, one of the two responses is sampled from this SFT model and the other from an off-policy pool model.

The 8B Tülu 3 SFT checkpoint generated the on-policy slice (one response per pair) of the preference data used to train allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-8B-DPO; the other response comes from one of the off-policy pool models.

description_variants: On-policy preference completions for the 70B mixture were generated from the 70B Tülu 3 SFT model.
On-policy preference responses (one of two responses per prompt) were generated by the Tulu 3 70B SFT model itself, then judged against off-policy generations.

allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-70B-SFT produced on-policy completions used in the chosen/rejected pairs of the 70B preference mixture for allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-70B-DPO (Stage 2 of the synthetic preference pipeline).
On-policy completions in the Tülu 3 70B preference mix are sampled from allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-70B-SFT per the synthetic preference pipeline in Section 5.2.1 'Response Generation'.

On-policy completions used in this preference mix (the SFT-Reused-On-Policy and WildChat-Reused-On-Policy splits) were sampled from allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-70B-SFT itself, then paired against off-policy completions from the model pool.
On-policy preference completions: one of the four candidates per prompt was sampled from Tülu 3 70B SFT (the SFT model that initializes this DPO run).
On-policy preference completions were generated by the Tulu 3 70B SFT model — paired against off-policy completions from external instruct models in the synthetic preference pipeline.

On-policy completions for allenai/llama-3.1-tulu-3-70b-preference-mixture were sampled from allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-70B-SFT (the Tülu 3 70B SFT model) and paired against off-policy outputs to produce the chosen/rejected pairs for DPO.

Tülu 3 70B SFT generates the on-policy half of preference pairs (Stage 2 of §5.2.1): for the on-policy subset, one of the two responses is sampled from this SFT model and the other from an off-policy pool model.

The 70B Tülu 3 SFT checkpoint generated the on-policy slice (one response per pair) of the preference data used to train allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-70B-DPO; the other response comes from one of the off-policy pool models.

edges.subject: allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-405B-DPO
edges.description: Same global GPT-4o-2024-0806 LLM judge applied to 405B preference annotation.
description_variants: Same global GPT-4o-2024-0806 LLM judge applied to 405B preference annotation.

Tulu 3 405B DPO's preference labels were obtained by GPT-4o-2024-08-06 acting as LLM-as-a-judge over four sampled responses per prompt, rating helpfulness/IF/honesty/truthfulness on a 1-5 scale; mean ratings binarized into chosen/rejected pairs.
Preference labels in the 405B preference mixture are produced by GPT-4o-2024-08-06 acting as LLM-as-a-judge per the same pipeline as 8B/70B (Section 5.2.1 Stage 3).

GPT-4o-2024-08-06 was the LLM-as-a-judge that rated 4 candidate completions per prompt across helpfulness/instruction-following/honesty/truthfulness; the highest-rated response became the chosen completion and a lower-rated one was sampled as rejected, producing the preference labels for this model's DPO mix.
GPT-4o-2024-08-06 LLM-as-judge rated candidate completions in the 405B preference mix (same pipeline as 8B/70B).

GPT-4o (gpt-4o-2024-08-06) acted as LLM-as-a-judge: rating each of the four sampled completions per prompt 1–5 along helpfulness/instruction-following/honesty/truthfulness, then binarizing into chosen/rejected pairs that became the 405B preference mixture used to DPO-tune allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-405B-DPO.
GPT-4o-2024-08-06 served as the LLM judge to rate preference pairs in the 405B preference mixture (per the global Tülu 3 recipe used for 8B/70B/405B).

Tulu 3 405B DPO preference pairs are labeled by GPT-4o-2024-08-06 acting as the LLM-as-a-judge across helpfulness, instruction-following, honesty, and truthfulness; the highest mean rating becomes the chosen response and a lower-rated response is the rejected.

edges.subject: allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-8B-SFT
edges.description: GPT-4o-2024-08-06 generated the IF-Persona-SFT, Persona MATH, Persona GSM, Persona Algebra prompts and responses, plus replacement responses for WildGuardMix and other persona-style training instances consumed by the 8B SFT.
description_variants: GPT-4o-2024-08-06 generated the IF-Persona-SFT, Persona MATH, Persona GSM, Persona Algebra prompts and responses, plus replacement responses for WildGuardMix and other persona-style training instances consumed by the 8B SFT.
Tulu 3 8B SFT trained on allenai/tulu-3-sft-personas-math, whose problems and CoT solutions were synthetically generated by GPT-4o-2024-08-06 conditioned on PersonaHub personas.
Tulu 3 8B SFT trains on the persona Python coding dataset (allenai/tulu-3-sft-personas-code) whose programming problems were generated by GPT-4o conditioned on personas.
Tulu 3 8B SFT trains on If-Persona-Sft (allenai/tulu-3-sft-personas-instruction-following), whose verifiable instruction-response pairs were synthesized by GPT-4o-2024-08-06.
For Tulu 3 8B SFT, the WildGuardMix component's responses were regenerated by GPT-4o (the original responses from a weaker model were discarded).
Assistant solutions in the persona-math SFT subset (which is part of the Tülu 3 8B SFT training mixture) were generated by GPT-4o.
Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-8B-SFT trained on the allenai/tulu-3-sft-personas-math constituent (149,960 hard-math word-problem prompts), whose assistant outputs were generated by GPT-4o.

Tülu 3 8B SFT trains on Persona-IF, Persona-MATH, Persona-GSM, Persona-Algebra, hardcoded, and other Tülu 3 synthetic responses generated by GPT-4o (gpt-4o-2024-08-06) per the persona-driven pipeline (§3.1.2, §4.1.1).

The Persona-IF SFT data (allenai/tulu-3-sft-personas-instruction-following — 29,980 verifiable instruction-following examples) consumed by this model was generated end-to-end by GPT-4o-2024-08-06: GPT-4o was given a persona, a data-synthesis prompt, and one seed verifiable instruction, and produced both the new instruction and its response.

The Persona-Math SFT data (allenai/tulu-3-sft-personas-math, allenai/tulu-3-sft-personas-math-grade, allenai/tulu-3-sft-personas-algebra — totaling ~220K math problems with multi-step solutions) was zero-shot generated by GPT-4o-2024-08-06 conditioned on Persona-Hub personas, both for the problems and the multi-step solutions.
The Persona-Code SFT data (allenai/tulu-3-sft-personas-code — 34,999 Python programming problems) had problem statements generated by GPT-4o-2024-08-06 conditioned on Persona-Hub personas.

Per Section 4.1.1, the WildGuardMix SFT responses used in this Tulu 3 SFT run were re-generated by GPT-4o (the original WildGuardMix responses came from a weaker model, so Tulu 3 generated fresh responses for these prompts).

GPT-4o produced assistant completions for the allenai/tulu-3-sft-personas-math SFT data (149,960 hard math word problems grounded in personas) that the 8B Tülu 3 SFT model trained on as part of the Tülu 3 SFT mixture.

GPT-4o-2024-08-06 generated synthetic prompts and/or responses in the Tülu 3 SFT mixture: persona-driven IF prompts/responses, math word-problem solutions, hardcoded responses, and regenerated WildGuardMix safety responses.

GPT-4o (gpt-4o-2024-08-06) generated SFT completions for prompts that lacked responses (Persona prompts, hardcoded prompts) and replaced responses from weaker models (e.g. WildGuardMix), comprising a substantial share of allenai/tulu-3-sft-mixture used to train Tülu 3 8B SFT.
GPT-4o generated assistant completions in allenai/tulu-3-sft-personas-math (149,960 hard math word problems, persona-grounded), a component of the Tulu-3 SFT data consumed by Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-8B-SFT.

GPT-4o-2024-08-06 generated all persona-driven prompts (IF, MATH, GSM, Algebra, Python prompts) and authored the responses for Persona MATH/GSM/Algebra/IF as well as regenerated responses for WildGuardMix; this synthetic data is part of the SFT mix.
GPT-4o was used as one of two LLM generators that produced assistant outputs for the Tulu 3 Persona MATH split (~149,960 prompts) included in this model's SFT mixture.

GPT-4o-2024-08-06 generated SFT responses for Persona-Math (~150K), Persona-GSM (~50K), Persona-Algebra (20K), Persona-IF (~30K), and replacement responses for WildGuardMix prompts. Used in Tulu 3 8B SFT mixture.

edges.subject: allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-70B-SFT
edges.description: Same global GPT-4o-driven persona synthesis pipeline produced data consumed by the 70B SFT.
description_variants: Same global GPT-4o-driven persona synthesis pipeline produced data consumed by the 70B SFT.
Tulu 3 70B SFT consumes the same persona-math content (allenai/tulu-3-sft-personas-math, allenai/tulu-3-sft-personas-math-grade, allenai/tulu-3-sft-personas-algebra) generated by GPT-4o.
Tulu 3 70B SFT trains on the persona Python coding dataset (allenai/tulu-3-sft-personas-code) whose programming problems were generated by GPT-4o conditioned on personas.
Tulu 3 70B SFT trains on If-Persona-Sft (allenai/tulu-3-sft-personas-instruction-following), whose verifiable instruction-response pairs were synthesized by GPT-4o-2024-08-06.
For Tulu 3 70B SFT, the WildGuardMix component's responses were regenerated by GPT-4o (the original responses from a weaker model were discarded).
Assistant solutions in the persona-math SFT subset (which is part of the Tülu 3 70B SFT training mixture) were generated by GPT-4o.
Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-70B-SFT trained on the allenai/tulu-3-sft-personas-math constituent (149,960 hard-math word-problem prompts), whose assistant outputs were generated by GPT-4o.

Tülu 3 70B SFT trains on Persona-IF, Persona-MATH, Persona-GSM, Persona-Algebra, hardcoded, and other Tülu 3 synthetic responses generated by GPT-4o (gpt-4o-2024-08-06) per the persona-driven pipeline (§3.1.2, §4.1.1).

The Persona-IF SFT data (allenai/tulu-3-sft-personas-instruction-following — 29,980 verifiable instruction-following examples) consumed by this model was generated end-to-end by GPT-4o-2024-08-06: GPT-4o was given a persona, a data-synthesis prompt, and one seed verifiable instruction, and produced both the new instruction and its response.

The Persona-Math SFT data (allenai/tulu-3-sft-personas-math, allenai/tulu-3-sft-personas-math-grade, allenai/tulu-3-sft-personas-algebra — totaling ~220K math problems with multi-step solutions) was zero-shot generated by GPT-4o-2024-08-06 conditioned on Persona-Hub personas, both for the problems and the multi-step solutions.
The Persona-Code SFT data (allenai/tulu-3-sft-personas-code — 34,999 Python programming problems) had problem statements generated by GPT-4o-2024-08-06 conditioned on Persona-Hub personas.

Per Section 4.1.1, the WildGuardMix SFT responses used in this Tulu 3 SFT run were re-generated by GPT-4o (the original WildGuardMix responses came from a weaker model, so Tulu 3 generated fresh responses for these prompts).

GPT-4o produced assistant completions for the allenai/tulu-3-sft-personas-math SFT data (149,960 hard math word problems grounded in personas) that the 70B Tülu 3 SFT model trained on as part of the Tülu 3 SFT mixture.

GPT-4o-2024-08-06 generated synthetic prompts and/or responses in the Tülu 3 SFT mixture: persona-driven IF prompts/responses, math word-problem solutions, hardcoded responses, and regenerated WildGuardMix safety responses.

GPT-4o (gpt-4o-2024-08-06) generated SFT completions for prompts that lacked responses (Persona prompts, hardcoded prompts) and replaced responses from weaker models (e.g. WildGuardMix), comprising a substantial share of allenai/tulu-3-sft-mixture used to train Tülu 3 70B SFT.
GPT-4o generated assistant completions in allenai/tulu-3-sft-personas-math (149,960 persona-grounded math word problems), part of the Tulu-3 SFT mixture consumed by Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-70B-SFT.

GPT-4o-2024-08-06 generated all persona-driven prompts (IF, MATH, GSM, Algebra, Python prompts) and authored the responses for Persona MATH/GSM/Algebra/IF as well as regenerated responses for WildGuardMix; this synthetic data is part of the SFT mix.
GPT-4o was used as one of two LLM generators that produced assistant outputs for the Tulu 3 Persona MATH split (~149,960 prompts) included in this model's SFT mixture.

GPT-4o-2024-08-06 generated SFT responses for Persona-Math (~150K), Persona-GSM (~50K), Persona-Algebra (20K), Persona-IF (~30K), and replacement responses for WildGuardMix prompts. Used in Tulu 3 70B SFT mixture.

edges.subject: allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-8B-SFT
edges.description: PopQA 15-shot (score 29.3) used to evaluate Tulu-3 8B SFT in the performance table.
description_variants: PopQA 15-shot (score 29.3) used to evaluate Tulu-3 8B SFT in the performance table.
Tulu 3 8B SFT evaluated on PopQA (15-shot multiturn ICL) as part of the Tülu 3 development evaluation suite (Tables 2, 5, 6, 23, 31).
allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-8B-SFT was evaluated on PopQA (15 shot) as reported in the Tulu 3 8B Performance table (column: Tülu 3 SFT 8B).
PopQA 15-shot multiturn-ICL — long-tail entity question answering.
allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-8B-SFT reports PopQA (15 shot) = 29.3 in the Tülu 3 8B Performance table; PopQA is one of the 13 benchmarks the Tülu 3 team uses for the 8B family release.
Tülu 3 8B SFT evaluated on PopQA (15-shot Multiturn ICL, EM) as part of the Tülu 3 development suite (Table 24).
PopQA 15-shot multiturn ICL, EM — entity-centric long-tail QA development eval.
Tülu 3 team evaluated allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-8B-SFT on PopQA (PopQA (15 shot)) and reported the score in the model card's main Performance table for the 8B family.
allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-8B-SFT is evaluated on akariasai/PopQA as part of the Tülu 3 development suite. Long-tail entity QA, 15-shot multi-turn ICL (Section 7.2).

description_variants: GPT-4o was one of the third-party generator models whose completions were sampled into the chosen/rejected pairs of the 70B preference mixture consumed by the Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-70B DPO step.

GPT-4o generated chosen/rejected completion candidates incorporated into allenai/tulu-3-sft-reused-off-policy, which is part of the Tulu 3 preference mixture used to DPO-align allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-70B-DPO. The off-policy preference dataset reuses prompts from the Tulu 3 SFT mixture and pairs them with completions sampled from a fixed roster of public instruct models.

GPT-4o generated chosen/rejected completion candidates incorporated into allenai/tulu-3-wildchat-unused, which is part of the Tulu 3 preference mixture used to DPO-align allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-70B-DPO. The wildchat-unused split pairs WildChat-1M prompts (not reused elsewhere in the Tulu 3 SFT mix) with completions sampled from the same roster of public instruct models used elsewhere in the Tulu 3 preference data.

GPT-4o generated chosen/rejected completion candidates incorporated into allenai/tulu-3-wildchat-ultrafeedback, which is part of the Tulu 3 preference mixture used to DPO-align allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-70B-DPO. The wildchat-ultrafeedback umbrella aggregates WildChat-IF and WildChat-reused on-policy splits with the wildchat-unused off-policy split; chosen/rejected pairs draw completions from the listed instruct models alongside on-policy Tulu 3 generations, with Ultrafeedback-style LLM-judge preference annotations.

GPT-4o generated chosen/rejected completion candidates incorporated into allenai/tulu-3-sft-prompts-ultrafeedback, which is part of the Tulu 3 preference mixture used to DPO-align allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-70B-DPO. The sft-prompts-ultrafeedback umbrella aggregates SFT-reused on-policy and IF-augmented on-policy splits (8B and 70B) with the off-policy SFT-reused split; completions are sampled from the listed instruct models plus on-policy Tulu 3 generations, with Ultrafeedback-style LLM-judge preference annotations.

Off-policy preference responses for the Tulu 3 70B preference mix were sampled from the 22-model external pool, including GPT-4o, with four responses per prompt randomly drawn from the pool (Ultrafeedback-style).

GPT-4o was one of the ~22 chat models used to generate chosen/rejected completion candidates in allenai/tulu-3-wildchat-if-on-policy-70b, a constituent of the Tulu 3 preference mixture used to DPO-train allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-70B-DPO.

GPT-4o was one of the ~22 chat models used to generate chosen/rejected completion candidates in allenai/tulu-3-wildchat-reused-on-policy-70b, a constituent of the Tulu 3 preference mixture used to DPO-train allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-70B-DPO.

GPT-4o was one of the ~22 chat models used to generate chosen/rejected completion candidates across the WildChat splits bundled in allenai/tulu-3-wildchat-ultrafeedback, which feeds the Tulu 3 preference mixture used to DPO-train allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-70B-DPO.

GPT-4o was one of the heterogeneous chat models used to produce chosen and rejected generation pairs in the allenai/llama-3.1-tulu-3-70b-preference-mixture, which was consumed by allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-70B-DPO during DPO training.
GPT-4o was sampled as one of the off-policy generators in the 22-model pool whose completions populated chosen/rejected candidates for the 70B Tülu 3 preference mixture (Stage 2 / Table 38).

GPT-4o was one of the response generators producing the chosen/rejected completion candidates that populate the allenai/tulu-3-sft-reused-off-policy preference dataset, which allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-70B-DPO trains on during DPO (off-policy: completions from this generator alone, no policy-model rollouts in this split).

GPT-4o was one of the response generators producing the chosen/rejected completion candidates that populate the allenai/tulu-3-sft-reused-on-policy-70b preference dataset, which allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-70B-DPO trains on during DPO.

GPT-4o was one of the response generators producing the chosen/rejected completion candidates that populate the allenai/tulu-3-IF-augmented-on-policy-70b preference dataset, which allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-70B-DPO trains on during DPO.

GPT-4o was one of the response generators producing the chosen/rejected completion candidates that populate the allenai/tulu-3-sft-prompts-ultrafeedback preference dataset, which allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-70B-DPO trains on during DPO.
Off-policy completions in the Tülu 3 70B preference mix include responses sampled from GPT-4o; OpenAI/gpt-4o is one of the ~22-model off-policy pool listed in Table 38.
GPT-4o was used to generate chosen and/or rejected completion candidates that compose the 337,186 preference pairs in the mixture used to DPO-train allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-70B-DPO.

GPT-4o was one of the panel generator models producing chosen/rejected response candidates for allenai/tulu-3-sft-reused-off-policy (Llama 3.1 Tulu 3 SFT reused, off-policy); outputs entered allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-70B-DPO's DPO preference training data via the synth_pref pipeline (Ultrafeedback template + LLM judge).

GPT-4o was one of the panel generator models producing chosen/rejected response candidates for allenai/tulu-3-sft-reused-on-policy-70b (Llama 3.1 Tulu 3 SFT reused, on-policy 70B); outputs entered allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-70B-DPO's DPO preference training data via the synth_pref pipeline (Ultrafeedback template + LLM judge).

GPT-4o was one of the panel generator models producing chosen/rejected response candidates for allenai/tulu-3-wildchat-if-on-policy-70b (Llama 3.1 Tulu 3 Wildchat IF, on-policy 70B); outputs entered allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-70B-DPO's DPO preference training data via the synth_pref pipeline (Ultrafeedback template + LLM judge).

GPT-4o was one of the panel generator models producing chosen/rejected response candidates for allenai/tulu-3-wildchat-reused-on-policy-70b (Llama 3.1 Tulu 3 Wildchat reused, on-policy 70B); outputs entered allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-70B-DPO's DPO preference training data via the synth_pref pipeline (Ultrafeedback template + LLM judge).

GPT-4o was one of the panel generator models producing chosen/rejected response candidates for allenai/tulu-3-IF-augmented-on-policy-70b (Llama 3.1 Tulu 3 IF-Augmented, on-policy 70B); outputs entered allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-70B-DPO's DPO preference training data via the synth_pref pipeline (Ultrafeedback template + LLM judge).

GPT-4o was one of the panel generator models producing chosen/rejected response candidates for allenai/tulu-3-ultrafeedback-cleaned-on-policy-70b (Llama 3.1 Tulu 3 Ultrafeedback Cleaned, on-policy 70B); outputs entered allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-70B-DPO's DPO preference training data via the synth_pref pipeline (Ultrafeedback template + LLM judge).

The rejected completions in Persona-IF-Pref (allenai/tulu-3-pref-personas-instruction-following), included in the 70B DPO mix, were generated by GPT-4o after rewriting each prompt to relax one constraint.

Off-policy completions in this DPO model's preference mix were sampled from the 22-model external pool that includes OpenAI/gpt-4o; for each prompt, 4 candidate completions were drawn from a random subset of the pool, then rated by GPT-4o.

Chosen and/or rejected completions in the allenai/llama-3.1-tulu-3-70b-preference-mixture preference pairs (used to DPO-train allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-70B-DPO) were generated by GPT-4o (OpenAI/gpt-4o).

OpenAI/gpt-4o (cited as 'GPT-4o') generated chosen / rejected completion candidates that populate Llama 3.1 Tulu 3 SFT reused (off-policy), the Tulu-3 preference-mixture component that allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-70B-DPO consumed during its DPO post-training stage.

OpenAI/gpt-4o (cited as 'GPT-4o') generated chosen / rejected completion candidates that populate Llama 3.1 Tulu 3 SFT reused (on-policy 70b), the Tulu-3 preference-mixture component that allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-70B-DPO consumed during its DPO post-training stage.

OpenAI/gpt-4o (cited as 'GPT-4o') generated chosen / rejected completion candidates that populate Llama 3.1 Tulu 3 IF-Augmented (on-policy 70B), the Tulu-3 preference-mixture component that allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-70B-DPO consumed during its DPO post-training stage.

OpenAI/gpt-4o (listed verbatim as 'GPT-4o') generated chosen and/or rejected completions in the bundled preference pairs of allenai/tulu-3-sft-prompts-ultrafeedback; these flow into allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-70B-DPO's DPO training.

OpenAI/gpt-4o (listed verbatim as 'GPT-4o') generated chosen/rejected completions for the WildChat+UltraFeedback preference pairs in allenai/tulu-3-wildchat-ultrafeedback; these flow into allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-70B-DPO's DPO training.

OpenAI/gpt-4o (listed verbatim as 'GPT-4o') was one of the response generators producing chosen and/or rejected completions inside the preference pairs of allenai/tulu-3-wildchat-unused; those synthetic completions enter allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-70B-DPO's DPO training.

OpenAI/gpt-4o (listed verbatim as 'GPT-4o') was one of the response generators producing chosen and/or rejected completions inside the preference pairs of allenai/tulu-3-wildchat-if-on-policy-70b; those synthetic completions enter allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-70B-DPO's DPO training.

OpenAI/gpt-4o (listed verbatim as 'GPT-4o') was one of the response generators producing chosen and/or rejected completions inside the preference pairs of allenai/tulu-3-wildchat-reused-on-policy-70b; those synthetic completions enter allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-70B-DPO's DPO training.

OpenAI/gpt-4o (listed verbatim as 'GPT-4o') was one of the response generators producing chosen and/or rejected completions inside the preference pairs of allenai/tulu-3-ultrafeedback-cleaned-on-policy-70b; those synthetic completions enter allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-70B-DPO's DPO training.
Off-policy candidate completions for the 70B DPO preference pairs were sampled from a 22-model pool (Table 38); 'OpenAI/gpt-4o' is one entry.
GPT-4o is one of the 22 off-policy chat models in the Tülu 3 preference-pipeline model pool that sampled responses for the 70B preference mixture used to DPO-tune allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-70B-DPO.

GPT-4o was one of 24 models used to generate chosen and/or rejected completions across the prompts in this preference mixture; the model card lists it among the generation pool whose outputs populate the chosen/rejected pairs.

GPT-4o generated chosen and/or rejected completion candidates populating the allenai/tulu-3-wildchat-if-on-policy-70b preference pairs consumed by allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-70B-DPO during DPO post-training.

GPT-4o generated chosen and/or rejected completion candidates populating the allenai/tulu-3-ultrafeedback-cleaned-on-policy-70b preference pairs consumed by allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-70B-DPO during DPO post-training.

GPT-4o generated chosen/rejected completion candidates populating the wildchat-ultrafeedback collection's preference pairs consumed by allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-70B-DPO via the Tulu 3 preference mixture.
GPT-4o generated chosen/rejected completion candidates populating the wildchat-unused preference pairs consumed by allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-70B-DPO via the Tulu 3 preference mixture.
GPT-4o generated chosen/rejected completion candidates in the tulu-3-sft-prompts-ultrafeedback preference dataset consumed by Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-70B-DPO.
GPT-4o is a member of the 22-model off-policy pool (Table 38) from which four candidate responses per prompt were sampled; these completions become chosen/rejected pairs for the 70B DPO mixture.

GPT-4o produced chosen/rejected completion candidates for the off-policy and 70B on-policy preference datasets that comprise the 70B Tulu-3 preference mixture used to DPO-align Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-70B-DPO.

GPT-4o (Hurst et al., 2024) sits in the 22-model Tülu 3 preference response pool: for each prompt four pool models are randomly sampled to produce candidate responses, which the GPT-4o judge then sorts into chosen/rejected pairs that train allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-70B-DPO.

GPT-4o contributed chosen and/or rejected response generations to the llama-3.1-tulu-3-70b-preference-mixture (70B preference mixture, used for DPO training of allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-70B-DPO); pairs were rated via the Ultrafeedback template by an LLM judge.

GPT-4o generated chosen/rejected completion candidates incorporated into allenai/tulu-3-wildchat-ultrafeedback, which is part of the Tulu 3 preference mixture used to DPO-align allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-8B-DPO. The wildchat-ultrafeedback umbrella aggregates WildChat-IF and WildChat-reused on-policy splits with the wildchat-unused off-policy split; chosen/rejected pairs draw completions from the listed instruct models alongside on-policy Tulu 3 generations, with Ultrafeedback-style LLM-judge preference annotations.

GPT-4o generated chosen/rejected completion candidates incorporated into allenai/tulu-3-sft-prompts-ultrafeedback, which is part of the Tulu 3 preference mixture used to DPO-align allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-8B-DPO. The sft-prompts-ultrafeedback umbrella aggregates SFT-reused on-policy and IF-augmented on-policy splits (8B and 70B) with the off-policy SFT-reused split; completions are sampled from the listed instruct models plus on-policy Tulu 3 generations, with Ultrafeedback-style LLM-judge preference annotations.

Off-policy preference responses for the Tulu 3 8B preference mix were sampled from the 22-model external pool, including GPT-4o, with four responses per prompt randomly drawn from the pool (Ultrafeedback-style).

edges.subject: Alibaba-NLP/WebShaper
edges.description: QwQ-32B serves as the trajectory-generation model: a ReAct agent built on QwQ rolls out 5 trajectories per question and yields the 5,000 ReAct trajectories used as SFT and RL training data.
description_variants: Same 5,000 ReAct trajectories generated by a QwQ-based agent are reused as SFT+RL training data for this variant. Note QwQ-32B simultaneously serves as the warm-start backbone and the upstream trajectory generator.

The 5,000-trajectory WebShaper training set is produced by a QwQ-32B-based ReAct agent (the same Expander backbone), which executes Search/Visit tool-calls to solve each expanded question; trajectories are then filtered for correctness and quality before SFT/RL.

anchor_list.excerpt: we instantiate an agent framework based on QwQ structurally aligned with the Expander, adopting the ReAct paradigm ... For each input question, we perform 5 times rollouts ... We finally obtain 5,000 trajectories for later supervised training and reinforcement learning.
anchor_list.explanation: Names QwQ as the trajectory-generating agent whose rollouts become WebShaper-32B's training data.

anchor_list.excerpt: we instantiate an agent framework based on QwQ structurally aligned with the Expander, adopting the ReAct paradigm
anchor_list.explanation: Documents the QwQ-based trajectory generator shared across all WebShaper training data.

anchor_list.excerpt: we instantiate an agent framework based on QwQ structurally aligned with the Expander, adopting the ReAct paradigm (Yao et al., 2023). At each timestep, the agent first first produces a Thought ... For each input question, we perform 5 times rollouts.
anchor_list.explanation: Names QwQ as the model that generates the WebShaper SFT/RL trajectories.

edges.subject: Alibaba-NLP/WebShaper
edges.description: During trajectory construction the Visit tool retrieves a web page (via Jina) and uses Qwen-2.5-72B as the summarization model that extracts goal-relevant information from each page; this rewritten content lives inside the trajectories used for training.
description_variants: Visit-tool page summaries (Qwen-2.5-72B) are baked into the trajectories that train the QwQ-32B WebShaper variant.

Qwen-2.5-72B summarizes Jina-fetched web pages into goal-conditioned excerpts that flow into the Expander's R-Union step (during dataset synthesis) and the agent's Visit observations (during trajectory rollouts) — i.e., it rewrites external web content rather than originating it, making it a content transformer for both the WebShaper dataset and the WebShaper agent's training trajectories.

anchor_list.excerpt: The full content of the page is first retrieved using Jina, after which a summarization model (Qwen-2.5-72B in our implementation) extracts information relevant to the specified goal.
anchor_list.explanation: Documents Qwen-2.5-72B as the page-content rewriter inside the trajectories that make up WebShaper-32B's training data.
anchor_list.position: Appendix A 'Agent Details', Visit tool description

anchor_list.excerpt: a summarization model (Qwen-2.5-72B in our implementation) extracts information relevant to the specified goal.
anchor_list.explanation: Qwen-2.5-72B is the page-content rewriter inside the trajectories used by every WebShaper variant.

anchor_list.excerpt: a summarization model (Qwen-2.5-72B in our implementation) extracts information relevant to the specified goal.
anchor_list.explanation: Names Qwen-2.5-72B as the summarization/transformation model for retrieved page content.

edges.subject: Alibaba-NLP/WebShaper
edges.description: QwQ-32B decides inclusion in three places of the WebShaper data pipeline: (1) seed-question filter inside the WebDancer rollout-correctness framework (5 rollouts per seed), (2) Validate tool inside the Expander (consistency + answerability checks per Eq.11), and (3) judge LLM filtering trajectories by final-answer correctness (Section 3.3).
description_variants: Seed-question rollout filter, Expander Validate tool, and trajectory correctness judge all use QwQ-32B; these gating decisions shape the training data ingested by the QwQ-32B variant.

QwQ-32B (run inside the WebDancer ReAct framework) performs 5 rollouts per candidate seed question and only questions for which at least one rollout returns the correct answer survive into the 18k seed pool that ultimately feeds WebShaper SFT/RL.

Inside the Expander loop, QwQ is invoked as the Validate tool to (1) check each generated sub-question's type-consistency with its constant and (2) verify the sub-question is non-trivial; questions failing either check are discarded, gating which expansions enter the WebShaper dataset.

anchor_list.excerpt: We complete all the seed questions by WebDancer framework based on the QwQ model. We perform 5 times rollouts for each question and keep the data where there must be as least one rollout correctly answering the question.
anchor_list.explanation: Names QwQ as the model whose rollout correctness gates inclusion of seed questions; the same QwQ powers the Validate tool and the judge.
anchor_list.position: Section 3.1 (seed filter) and Section 3.2.3 'Expander Agent' (Validate) and Section 3.3 (correctness judge)

anchor_list.excerpt: We complete all the seed questions by WebDancer framework based on the QwQ model. We perform 5 times rollouts for each question and keep the data where there must be as least one rollout correctly answering the question.
anchor_list.explanation: QwQ-driven filtering decisions gate which seed questions and trajectories enter the WebShaper training set.

anchor_list.excerpt: We complete all the seed questions by WebDancer framework (Wu et al., 2025a) based on the QwQ model (Team, 2025). We perform 5 times rollouts for each question and keep the data where there must be as least one rollout correctly answering the question.
anchor_list.explanation: States QwQ-driven rollouts decide seed-question inclusion (filter, not generation).

anchor_list.excerpt: We call QwQ once time per each purpose. In the first consistency validation ... For the second validation, we require QwQ to answer the sub-question. If the prediction is the same as C, we regard it as invalid.
anchor_list.explanation: Documents QwQ as the validator deciding inclusion of synthesized sub-questions.

description_variants: WebShaper-72B is trained on the WebShaper dataset of formalization-driven, agentically synthesized information-seeking question-answer pairs.

anchor_list.excerpt: We leverage the proposed framework to construct the WebShaper dataset, which serves as training data for the IS agent.
anchor_list.explanation: Names the synthesized WebShaper dataset as the training corpus shared across the 32B and 72B WebShaper agents.

anchor_list.excerpt: WebShaper is a dataset for training information-seeking agents, we release 500 questions-answer pairs in HuggingFace... You can access the dataset directly via data/webshaper.500.jsonl.
anchor_list.explanation: States the WebShaper dataset is for training IS agents, which the README's experiments evaluate as WebShaper-32B/72B.

edges.subject: WebShaper-72B
edges.description: Like the 32B variant, WebShaper-72B's training data originates from random walks over an offline Wikipedia article database used as the seed-question source.

anchor_list.excerpt: we constructed an offline Wikipedia database by downloading all URLs corresponding to Wikipedia articles ... we perform random walks across these articles
anchor_list.explanation: Pipes Wikipedia content into the training corpus consumed by every WebShaper agent variant.

edges.subject: WebShaper-72B
edges.description: QwQ-32B is the ReAct trajectory generator: 5,000 trajectories rolled out by a QwQ-based agent become the SFT+RL training data also used for the 72B variant.

anchor_list.excerpt: we instantiate an agent framework based on QwQ structurally aligned with the Expander, adopting the ReAct paradigm ... We finally obtain 5,000 trajectories for later supervised training and reinforcement learning.
anchor_list.explanation: Names QwQ as the trajectory-generating agent whose rollouts become the 72B variant's training data.

edges.subject: WebShaper-72B
edges.description: The Visit tool used during trajectory construction summarizes web-page content with Qwen-2.5-72B; this rewritten content lives inside the trajectories used to train WebShaper-72B.

anchor_list.excerpt: a summarization model (Qwen-2.5-72B in our implementation) extracts information relevant to the specified goal.
anchor_list.explanation: Confirms Qwen-2.5-72B is the page-content rewriter shared across the trajectories that train every WebShaper variant.
anchor_list.position: Appendix A 'Agent Details', Visit tool description

edges.subject: WebShaper-72B
edges.description: QwQ-32B decides inclusion at three points of the upstream pipeline (seed-question rollout filter, Expander Validate tool, trajectory-correctness judge) for the 72B variant's training data.

anchor_list.excerpt: We complete all the seed questions by WebDancer framework based on the QwQ model. We perform 5 times rollouts for each question and keep the data where there must be as least one rollout correctly answering the question.
anchor_list.explanation: Same QwQ-based filter and judge gate the training data ingested by the 72B variant.

edges.subject: Alibaba-NLP/WebShaper

WebWalkerQA is used as an alternative 5k SFT data variant in the WebShaper team's data-comparison ablation; matched-backbone WebShaper consistently outperforms it on GAIA (e.g., 43.6 vs 32.0 on Qwen-2.5-32B).

edges.subject: allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-8B-DPO
edges.description: Prompts in the allenai/tulu-3-wildchat-if-on-policy-8b sub-corpus (10,792 pairs) — a constituent of the allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-8B-DPO DPO mixture — are sourced from WildChat (allenai/WildChat-1M).
description_variants: Prompts in the allenai/tulu-3-wildchat-if-on-policy-8b sub-corpus (10,792 pairs) — a constituent of the allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-8B-DPO DPO mixture — are sourced from WildChat (allenai/WildChat-1M).

Prompts in the allenai/tulu-3-wildchat-reused-on-policy-8b sub-corpus (17,207 pairs) — a constituent of the allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-8B-DPO DPO mixture — are sourced from WildChat (allenai/WildChat-1M).

Tulu 3 8B DPO trained on the 'WildChat IF (sub-corpus: WildChat prompts containing IF constraints, extracted via GPT-4)' (10,792 prefs) component of the 8B preference mixture. Pairs were generated via the synthetic UltraFeedback-style pipeline using a 22-model pool (off-policy) plus on-policy completions from Tulu 3 8B SFT, with GPT-4o-2024-08-06 as the LLM judge.

edges.subject: allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-8B-DPO
edges.description: The pref-IF preference dataset is constructed by rewriting prompts from allenai/tulu-3-sft-personas-instruction-following to relax one constraint per prompt, so the dataset's design is directly derived from the SFT version's constraint-grounded prompts.
description_variants: The pref-IF preference dataset is constructed by rewriting prompts from allenai/tulu-3-sft-personas-instruction-following to relax one constraint per prompt, so the dataset's design is directly derived from the SFT version's constraint-grounded prompts.

The preference dataset allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-8B-DPO consumed was constructed by reworking prompts from the SFT-stage allenai/tulu-3-sft-personas-instruction-following dataset; methodology borrowed from that SFT recipe (rewrite to relax one constraint to produce a rejected response).

The preference dataset allenai/tulu-3-pref-personas-instruction-following that allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-8B-DPO trained on was constructed by taking a subset of the SFT-stage dataset allenai/tulu-3-sft-personas-instruction-following and rewriting prompts to relax one of the given constraints; the response to the modified prompt is treated as the rejected completion. This reuses the persona-IF data recipe at the DPO stage.

anchor_list.excerpt: we took a subset of its supervised-tuning version [here](https://huggingface.co/datasets/allenai/tulu-3-sft-personas-instruction-following) and convert it into preference dataset. Specifically, we rewrote each prompt in the subset to relax one of the given constraints
anchor_list.explanation: Card explicitly states pref-IF was constructed by rewriting prompts from the SFT-personas-IF dataset, indicating methodological derivation.

edges.subject: allenai/Olmo-3-32B-Instruct-DPO
edges.description: Delta-aware GPT-judged preference pairs are produced by sampling four model completions per prompt and judging them with a GPT-4.1 judge using the UltraFeedback judge prompts; the worst is selected as rejected and the best as chosen.

anchor_list.excerpt: Our initial attempts to modernize the UltraFeedback pipeline from OLMo 2 and Tülu 3 by improving the quality of the LLM judge (GPT-4o →GPT-4.1)
anchor_list.explanation: Section 5.3.1 names GPT-4.1 as the LLM judge that scores DPO completions.

anchor_list.excerpt: we sample four model completions and judge them via a GPT-4.1 judge with the UltraFeedback judge prompts
anchor_list.explanation: A.7.4 details the GPT-4.1 UltraFeedback-style judging procedure for Dolci Instruct DPO.

edges.subject: nvidia/Nemotron-3-Nano-Omni-30B-A3B-Reasoning-BF16
edges.description: QwQ-32B was one of two reasoning LLMs used to generate the 19.4B-token InfiniByte cross-domain composite reasoning split of Nemotron-Pretraining-Specialized-v1.
description_variants: QwQ-32B was one of two reasoning LLMs used to generate the 19.4B-token InfiniByte cross-domain composite reasoning split of Nemotron-Pretraining-Specialized-v1.

QwQ-32B co-generates the Synthetic InfiniByte-Reasoning subset (19.4B tokens) of Nemotron-Pretraining-Specialized-v1 alongside Qwen3-235B-A22B-Thinking-2507 — multi-step composite problems formed by cross-breeding code, math, physics, chemistry, and scientific corpora.

Qwen/QwQ-32B co-generated the Nemotron-Pretraining-InfiniByte-Reasoning subset (19.4B tokens of procedurally cross-bred multi-step composite problems spanning code, math, physics, chemistry, and scientific corpora) of Nemotron-Pretraining-Specialized-v1, alongside Qwen3-235B-A22B-Thinking-2507. These tokens enter Nemotron 3 Nano pretraining.
QwQ-32B generates cross-domain multi-step reasoning problems for the 19.4B-token Nemotron-Pretraining-InfiniByte-Reasoning subset of Specialized-v1, consumed by Nemotron 3 Nano pretraining.

edges.subject: Qwen/Qwen3-8B
edges.description: Qwen3-8B is SFT-trained on the Nemotron-Math-v2 dataset using the same recipe as the 30B-A3B run with adjusted parallelism.
description_variants: Qwen3-8B is SFT-trained on the Nemotron-Math-v2 dataset using the same recipe as the 30B-A3B run with adjusted parallelism.

Qwen3-8B is supervised fine-tuned on the same Nemotron-Math-v2 corpus as Qwen3-30B-A3B for the scaling-study experiments; under the high reasoning mode with Python TIR, the resulting Qwen3-8B reaches 100% maj@16 accuracy on AIME24 and AIME25.

In the Nemotron-Math-v2 paper (arXiv:2512.15489) Qwen3-8B is fine-tuned (ns sft_nemo_rl, megatron backend) on the Nemotron-Math-v2 dataset (350K problems × 7.5M gpt-oss-120b solutions across high/medium/low reasoning effort, with and without Python TIR) as a paper demonstration.

anchor_list.excerpt: Both Qwen3-8B and Qwen3-30B-A3B achieve 100% maj@16 accuracy on AIME24/25 under the Python TIR high reasoning mode.
anchor_list.explanation: Confirms Qwen3-8B was fine-tuned on Nemotron-Math and evaluated under the same protocol.

gpt-oss-120b generated all 7.5M natural-language solutions in Nemotron-Math-v2 (8 with-Python and 8 no-Python solutions per problem, in three reasoning_effort regimes) that Qwen3-8B trains on.

edges.subject: Qwen/Qwen3-8B
edges.description: Qwen3-8B is fine-tuned on Nemotron-Math-Proofs-v1 verified-proof conversations as SFT data.
description_variants: Qwen3-8B is fine-tuned on Nemotron-Math-Proofs-v1 verified-proof conversations as SFT data.

Qwen3-8B is fine-tuned (ns sft_nemo_rl, 1000 steps, lr 1e-4, max sequence length 49152, TP=4 CP=4) on Nemotron-Math-Proofs-v1's verified proof conversations as a paper demonstration; the fine-tuned model achieves 85.3% / 90.2% pass@32 on miniF2F (without/with self-correction).

anchor_list.explanation: Recipe explicitly fine-tunes Qwen3-8B on the Nemotron-Math-Proofs SFT data (the messages field of the dataset).

edges.subject: Qwen/Qwen2.5-3B-Instruct
edges.description: SFT training data is the 3,202 multi-hop subset of TaskCraft synthetic agentic tasks plus execution trajectories produced by the TaskCraft workflow.
description_variants: The SFT phase atop Search-R1 RL uses the same 3,202 multi-hop TaskCraft synthetic tasks and execution trajectories.

Qwen2.5-3B-Instruct is supervised-fine-tuned on 3,202 synthesized multi-hop tasks and execution trajectories drawn from the TaskCraft dataset, with content masking applied to search-tool contexts during training (Section 4.4).

edges.subject: Qwen/Qwen2.5-3B-Instruct
edges.description: The TaskCraft synthetic tasks and trajectories used for SFT are produced by the TaskCraft workflow whose default LLM is GPT-4.1 (used as candidate-answer extractor, question generator, depth/width merger, and judge-LLM verifier).
description_variants: The TaskCraft trajectories used in the SFT phase are produced by the TaskCraft workflow whose default LLM is GPT-4.1.

The TaskCraft synthetic tasks/trajectories used to SFT Qwen2.5-3B-Instruct were produced by the TaskCraft automated workflow whose underlying LLM is GPT-4.1 (identified as the 'LLM only' atomic-task generator in Section 4.5 / Table 4 against which the workflow is compared).
